---
title: "Pipeline Overview"
---

## Summary

This project is a Shiny application that pulls live CDC mortality data, builds trend and comparison views, and generates a plain-language interpretation using a large language model. The data are fetched on demand from CDC public datasets via the Socrata API.

## Data Sources

- NCHS Leading Causes of Death: United States (1999–2020, all states)
- VSRR Provisional Drug Overdose Death Counts (2015–present, monthly)

## Data Ingestion

- HTTP requests are made with `httr2`.
- JSON responses are parsed with `jsonlite` and converted into tibbles.
- No CDC token is required to access these open datasets.

## Processing and Metrics

Key transformations in `R/clean_data.R` include:

- Yearly trend aggregation for deaths and rates
- Percent change from a baseline year
- Cause comparison rankings
- State summaries for map views
- KPI headline statistics
- Overdose monthly and yearly summaries
- Structured text block for LLM prompting

## LLM Interpretation

A structured data summary is passed into `R/llm_summary.R` which builds a domain-specific prompt for a public health interpretation. The response is displayed in the app as a 4–6 sentence summary.

## Outputs

The app exposes two primary views:

- Mortality trends with multiple tabs for time series, geography, comparison, and raw data
- Overdose deep dive with monthly trends and state comparisons

## Project Structure

```text
new_file/
+-- README.md
+-- .env.example
+-- .gitignore
+-- _quarto.yml
+-- index.qmd
+-- report.qmd
+-- run-all.R
+-- styles.css
+-- data/
+-- R/
    +-- app.R
    +-- cdc_api.R
    +-- clean_data.R
    +-- llm_summary.R
    +-- plots.R
```
